prompt: Create a stakeholder register for an ERP migration affecting Sales, Finance, IT, and Operations departments. Executive sponsor is the COO.
assertions: identifies stakeholders across all 4 departments plus executive level
includes both internal and external stakeholders (e.g., ERP vendor)
every stakeholder has a unique ID (SH-XXX format)
power/interest classification applied to each stakeholder
engagement strategy defined for at least top 10 stakeholders
produces a power/interest matrix visualization
COO identified as Manage Closely / Definitive stakeholder

prompt: Two stakeholders have conflicting interests — the CFO wants to minimize cost and the CTO wants best-of-breed technology. How do I handle this in the register?
assertions: documents both perspectives without averaging or dismissing either
identifies this as a stakeholder conflict requiring facilitation
recommends alignment workshop or structured decision process
does NOT try to resolve the conflict by picking a side
records both positions with evidence tags [STAKEHOLDER]

prompt: Map our stakeholders using the Mitchell salience model.
assertions: explains the three dimensions: power, legitimacy, urgency
scores each stakeholder on all three dimensions
classifies into salience types (Definitive, Dominant, Dependent, etc.)
identifies dynamic salience — stakeholders who change type across phases
prioritizes engagement based on salience classification

prompt: We discovered a key regulatory stakeholder 3 months into the project. Update the register.
assertions: flags this as a stakeholder identification gap — should have been caught earlier
adds the regulator with full classification (likely Dormant→Definitive)
assesses impact on existing project plans
recommends updating the engagement calendar with regulatory touchpoints
documents the lesson learned for future risk register update
